steps.text: Measure exhaust gas moisture.
Check gauge calibration quarterly.
Check transducer calibration monthly.

preconditions.text: Moisture measurement is needed to correct the CO, formaldehyde, THC and/or O2 measurements to a dry basis.

constraints.text: Use a pressure sensor with measurement sensitivity of 0.002 inches water.

steps.text: Submit a rule report to each house of the Congress and to the Comptroller General of the United States.
Amend appendix A–3 to part 60 by revising figure 4–3 under the heading ‘‘18.0 Tables, Diagrams, Flowcharts, and Validation Data’’ in method 4.
Amend appendix A–4 to part 60 by revising section 10.1.3 in method 7.

Multiply the absorbance value obtained for each standard by the Kc factor (reciprocal of the least squares slope) to determine the distance each calibration point lies from the theoretical calibration line.
Amend appendix A–7 to part 60 by revising equation 19–5 in section 12.2.3.2 in method 19, adding sections 12.9 and 12.9.1 through 12.9.16 in method 25, and revising figure 25–6.

constraints.text: The difference between the calculated concentration values and the actual concentrations (i.e., 100, 200, 300, and 400 mg NO2) shall be less than 7 percent for all standards.

steps.text: Select and test an opacity monitor representative of a group for conformance with design specifications in ASTM D6216-20.
Test each opacity monitor for conformance with manufacturer's performance specifications in ASTM D6216-20.
Purchase an opacity monitor that meets the specifications of ASTM D6216-20, including a suitable data recorder or automated data acquisition handling system.
Obtain a certificate of conformance from the opacity monitor manufacturer.

preconditions.text: If the initial certification of the opacity monitor occurred before May 30, 2023, using D6216–98, D6216–03, D6216–07, or D6216–12, it is not necessary to recertify using D6216–20.

steps.text: Purchase an opacity monitor that complies with ASTM D6216–20.
Obtain a certificate of conformance from the opacity monitor manufacturer.
Simultaneously measure the opacities at the two locations or paths for a minimum period of time (e.g., 180-minutes) covering the range of normal operating conditions and compare the results.
Conduct a three-point calibration error test using three calibration attenuators that produce outlet pathlength corrected, single-pass opacity values shown in ASTM D6216–20, section 7.5.
Confirm the external audit device produces the proper zero value on the COMS data recorder.
Separately, insert each calibration attenuators (low, mid, and high-level) into the external audit device.

preconditions.text: If selecting alternative locations and light beam paths, demonstrate equivalence to the satisfaction of the Administrator or delegated agent.
If your applicable limit is less than 10 percent opacity, use attenuators as described in ASTM D6216–20, section 7.5 for applicable standards of 10 to 19 percent opacity.

constraints.text: The opacity at the alternative location is considered equivalent if (1) the average opacity value measured at the alternative location is within ±10 percent of the average opacity value measured at the location meeting the installation criteria, and (2) the difference between any two average opacity values is less than 2 percent opacity (absolute).

steps.text: Calculate the CERMS Relative Accuracy (RA) expressed as a percentage using equation 2–6 of section 12 of PS 2.
Substitute the applicable emission standard value in the denominator of equation 2–6 in place of the average RM value.

preconditions.text: The average emissions for the test are less than 50 percent of the applicable emission standard.

constraints.text: The RA of the CERMS shall be no greater than 20.0 percent in terms of the units of the emission standard.
The RA of the CERMS shall be no greater than 10.0 percent consistent with section 13.2 of PS 2.

steps.text: Select and use Method 29, Method 30A, Method 30B, or ASTM Method D6784-16 as the Reference Method (RM) for Hg concentration.
Obtain data derived with Method 4 during the RA test.
Conduct a minimum of nine RM test runs.
Correlate the CEMS and the RM test data as to the time and duration by determining the integrated average pollutant concentration for each RM test period from the CEMS final output.

preconditions.text: If Method 29, Method 30B, or ASTM D6784-16 is used, conduct the RM test runs with paired or duplicate sampling systems and use the average of the vapor phase Hg concentrations.
If the RM and CEMS measure on a different moisture basis.

If more than nine sets of RM test runs are performed, ensure the total number of RM test run results used to determine CEMS RA is greater than or equal to nine, and report all data including excluded data.

constraints.text: For Method 29 and ASTM Method D6784-16, the filterable portion of the sample need not be included when making comparisons to the CEMS results.
When Method 29, Method 30B, or ASTM D6784-16 is used, only test runs for which the paired RM trains meet the relative deviation criteria (RD) must be used in the RA calculations.
For Method 29 and ASTM D6784-16, use a minimum sample time of 2 hours; for Methods 30A and 30B, use a minimum sample time of 30 minutes.

steps.text: Calculate the RD.
Use these procedures to certify your PEMS after initial installation.
Certify your PEMS periodically thereafter to ensure the PEMS is operating properly.
Test the diluent component.

preconditions.text: Paired samples are taken using RM 30B, RM 29, or ASTM D6784–16.
Your PEMS contains a diluent (O2 or CO2) measuring component.

You, the source owner or operator, intend to use (with any necessary approvals) a predictive emission monitoring system (PEMS) to show compliance with your emission limitation under this part or 40 CFR part 61 or 63.

constraints.text: Express RD as a percentage or, for very low RM concentrations (≤1.0 μg/m3), as the absolute difference between Ca and Cb.

steps.text: Perform Sensor Evaluation Alert Test (optional).
Revise sections 4.1, 5.2.3, and 6.2 in procedure 1 of appendix F to part 60.
Revise section 2.5 in procedure 5 of appendix F to part 60.
Revise the heading for section 4.0 and add section 4.4 in procedure 5 of appendix F to part 60.
Revise section 5.1.3 in procedure 5 of appendix F to part 60.

preconditions.text: After each PEMS training.

constraints.text: PEMS Relative Accuracy (RA) must not exceed 10 percent if measurements are greater than 100 ppm (or 0.2 lbs/mm Btu), 20 percent if between 10 ppm and 100 ppm, or absolute mean difference must not exceed 2 ppm if below 10 ppm.

Relative Accuracy Audits (RAA) average difference must not exceed 10 percent for concentrations greater than 100 ppm, 20 percent for concentrations between 10 ppm and 100 ppm, or 2 ppm for concentrations at 20 ppm or less (1 percent for diluent PEMS).

steps.text: Follow the RATA test procedures in section 8.5 of PS 12A in appendix B to this part, except that only three test runs are required.
Calculate the relative accuracy according to equation 1–1 of Procedure 1 of this appendix.

preconditions.text: A RAA may be conducted in three of four calendar quarters, but in no more than three quarters in succession, as an alternative to the QGA.

constraints.text: The absolute value of the difference between the Hg CEMS output response and the reference gas must be less than or equal to 10.0 percent of the reference gas value or 0.8 mg/scm.

steps.text: Filter the sample through the filter paper, into an Erlenmeyer flask by applying a vacuum to the flask sidearm.
Replace filter paper as often as needed in order to maintain filter times of less than approximately 30 seconds per filter paper.
Seed dilution water with 1 milliliter of final effluent per liter of dilution water.
Adjust dilution ratios to reflect the lower oxygen demand of the filtered sample in comparison to the total BOD5, if required.
Perform method 5210B on the filtrate obtained.

preconditions.text: To determine soluble BOD5 in the effluent stream from an open biological treatment unit used to comply with §§ 63.446(e)(2) and 63.453(j)

constraints.text: Minimize the time for which vacuum is applied to prevent stripping of volatile organics from the sample.
No rinsing of sample container or filter bowl into the Erlenmeyer flask is allowed.
Three BOD bottles and different dilutions shall be used for each sample.

steps.text: Conduct the RATA as described in the RA test procedure (or alternate procedures section) described in the applicable performance specifications.
Analyze the appropriate performance audit samples received from the EPA as described in the applicable sampling methods.
Conduct an ACA as described in the calibration error (CE) test procedure described in the applicable performance specifications.
Cease hazardous waste burning immediately.

preconditions.text: When a performance test is also required under § 63.1207 to document compliance with emission standards, the RATA must coincide with the performance test.
Except in a quarter when a RATA is conducted instead.
If the RA from the RATA or the CE from the ACA exceeds the criteria in the applicable performance specifications.

constraints.text: Hazardous waste burning must cease immediately if the RA from the RATA or the CE from the ACA exceeds the criteria in the applicable performance specifications.

steps.text: Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure O2 at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure moisture content at the inlet and outlet of the control device.

preconditions.text: Moisture content measurement is needed to determine formaldehyde or THC and O2 concentrations on a dry basis.

constraints.text: For formaldehyde, THC, O2, and moisture measurement, ducts ≤6 inches in diameter may be sampled at a single point located at the duct centroid and ducts >6 and ≤12 inches in diameter may be sampled at 3 traverse points located at 16.7, 50.0, and 83.3% of the measurement line. If the duct is >12 inches in diameter and the sampling port location meets the two and half-diameter criterion of section 11.1.1 of method 1 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A, the duct may be sampled at ‘3-point long line’; otherwise, conduct the stratification testing and select sampling points according to section 8.1.2 of method 7E of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A.
Measure O2 using Method 3 or 3A or 3B of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–2, or ASTM D6522–00 (Reapproved 2005) (heated probe not necessary).
Measurements to determine O2 concentration must be made at the same time as the measurements for formaldehyde or THC concentration.
Measure moisture content using Method 4 of 40 CFR part 60, appendix A–3, or method 320 of 40 CFR part 63, appendix A, or ASTM D.
Measurements to determine moisture content must be made at the same time and location as the measurements for CO concentration.

steps.text: Measure moisture content at the inlet and outlet of the control device as needed to determine formaldehyde or THC and O2 concentrations on a dry basis using Method 4, Method 320, or ASTM D6348-03.
Measure formaldehyde at the inlet and the outlet of the control device using Method 320 or 323, or ASTM D6348-03.
Measure THC at the inlet and the outlet of the control device using Method 25A, reported as propane.
Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the exhaust of the stationary RICE.

preconditions.text: If demonstrating compliance with the formaldehyde percent reduction requirement
If demonstrating compliance with the THC percent reduction requirement

constraints.text: Measurements to determine moisture content must be made at the same time and location as the measurements for formaldehyde or THC concentration.
If using ASTM D6348-03 Annex A5 (Analyte Spiking Technique), the percent R must be greater than or equal to 70 and less than or equal to 130.
Formaldehyde concentration must be at 15 percent O2, dry basis. Results of this test consist of the average of the three 1-hour or longer runs.
THC concentration must be at 15 percent O2, dry basis. Results of this test consist of the average of the three 1-hour or longer runs.

For formaldehyde, CO, O2, and moisture measurement, ducts <= 6 inches in diameter may be sampled at a single point located at the duct centroid and ducts > 6 and <= 12 inches in diameter may be sampled at 3 traverse points located at 16.7, 50.0, and 83.3% of the measurement line.

steps.text: Revise table 5 to subpart UUUUU of part 63.
Comply with the requirements for performance testing for existing, new, or reconstructed affected sources.

preconditions.text: The capture system meets the criteria in method 204 of appendix M to 40 CFR part 51 for a permanent total enclosure (PE) and directs all the exhaust gases from the enclosure to an add-on control device.
